stem:
Which of the following expressions is(are) a factor of 2x² + 11x − 21?

I. x − 7

II. 2x − 3
rationale: Factor 2x² + 11x − 21 by looking for two numbers whose product is 2(−21) = −42 and whose sum is 11; these are 14 and −3. Splitting the middle term and factoring by grouping gives 2x² + 14x − 3x − 21 = 2x(x + 7) − 3(x + 7) = (x + 7)(2x − 3). So II is a factor, but I (x − 7) is not. The answer is II only.
